name: Boucle de Lourdes par le Pic du Jer
region_intro: Mountain biking around Lourdes offers diverse terrain at the foothills of the Pyrenees mountains. The region features a dramatic landscape with significant elevation changes, including the prominent Pic du Jer. Riders can explore lush river valleys like the Gave de Pau, glacial lakes, and challenging mountain trails. This area provides a comprehensive network of routes suitable for various skill levels, from gentle riverside paths to technical descents. Best mountain bike trails around Lourdes The most popular mountain bike trail is Boucle de Lourdes par le Pic du Jer, a 16.6 miles (26.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 16 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes significant climbs and descents around the Pic du Jer. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Voie Verte des Gaves and Ségus Loop, a difficult 17.0 miles (27.3 km) path. This route combines sections of the converted railway line with more challenging terrain through the Ségus area. Local mountain bikers also love the Trails Along the Gave de Pau – Bridge over the Gave de Pau loop from Lourdes, a 19.7 miles (31.7 km) trail leading through the scenic Gave de Pau valley, often completed in about 1 hour 56 minutes. Mountain biking around Lourdes is defined by the Pyrenees foothills, the Pic du Jer, and the Gave de Pau river valley. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Lourdes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 1000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Lourdes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Lourdes?
A: Lourdes offers a comprehensive network of over 60 mountain bike trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents in the Pyrenees foothills.

Q: Are there easy or family-friendly mountain bike trails in Lourdes?
A: Yes, Lourdes has several easy and family-friendly options. The region offers introductory circuits along the banks of the Gave de Pau, which are suitable for children. The "Voie Verte des Gaves," a converted railway line, also provides a gentle path for relaxed rides. While many routes are more challenging, you can find 6 easy trails and 16 moderate trails in the area.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Lourdes?
A: The terrain around Lourdes is diverse, ranging from lush river valleys like the Gave de Pau to significant elevation changes in the Pyrenees foothills. You'll encounter everything from smooth paths along the "Voie Verte" to technical descents and challenging climbs, especially around prominent features like the Pic du Jer.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Lourdes?
A: Yes, many routes around Lourdes are circular. For example, the moderate route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38572708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trails Along the Gave de Pau – Bridge over the Gave de Pau loop from Lourdes&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic circular ride through the valley. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e967682234/voie-verte-des-gaves-and-segus-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Voie Verte des Gaves and Ségus Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Lourdes offers numerous difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e967428957/boucle-de-lourdes-par-le-pic-du-jer" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boucle de Lourdes par le Pic du Jer&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding trail with significant climbs and descents. The Pic du Jer itself is renowned for downhill mountain biking, including technical red and black trails. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15012433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;River Crossing at Paréac – Single from Loumez loop from Adé&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What attractions or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails offer views of significant landmarks. The Pic du Jer is a major highlight, accessible by funicular, offering panoramic views of Lourdes and the Pyrenees. You can find trails that pass by or offer views of other peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2831019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pibeste Peak&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/764243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from the Summit of Béout&lt;/a&gt;. The glacial Lake of Lourdes is also a tranquil spot near some routes.

Q: Is there downhill mountain biking available in Lourdes?
A: Yes, the Pic du Jer is a prime location for downhill mountain biking. It features three distinct trails: an easy blue trail, a technical red trail, and difficult black and DH trails. The Pic du Jer has even hosted UCI DH Mountain Bike World Cups, highlighting its challenging nature. A funicular allows easy access to the summit with your bike.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Lourdes?
A: The mountain bike trails around Lourdes are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.2 stars from over 80 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from scenic river valleys to challenging mountain ascents, and the stunning Pyrenean backdrop.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that feature waterfalls or gorges?
A: While specific trails are not named for waterfalls, the region is known for its natural beauty. The road leading to attractions like Pont d'Espagne, which is accessible from the Lourdes area, is dotted with picturesque waterfalls and tumultuous gorges, adding to the scenic experience of the wider area.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Lourdes?
A: The region is generally suitable for mountain biking from spring through autumn. The Pyrenees foothills offer pleasant conditions outside of the winter months when some higher elevation trails might be affected by snow. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Can I access mountain bike trails using public transport in Lourdes?
A: Lourdes is well-connected, and some areas like the Pic du Jer are accessible via funicular, which can transport bikes. Additionally, a "Bike Bus" service is available to transport bikes to nearby specialized trail areas like Barèges Bike Park, making it easier to explore without a car.
tips: Located at an altitude of 1000 meters, this summit dominating Lourdes is a popular spot for downhill mountain biking. Equipped with a funicular, the Pic du Jer offers 3 downhill runs, from initiation to the most challenging and technical!
Trails all along the Gave and on the other side the cycle path! A perfect place to swim after an outing!

waypoints.name: Trails Along the Gave de Pau
